name: Certificate of Indigency
shortDescription: This proves that you have little income and need help for school or medical support.

details.longDescription: A Certificate of Indigency is a document issued by the barangay certifying that the holder is a resident of the barangay and is considered one of the less fortunate or low-income families in the community. It is often used to avail of government services and aid.

name: Barangay Certificate
shortDescription: Certifies that you are a resident of the barangay and a good resident.

details.longDescription: A Barangay Certificate is often required for various transactions to prove your residency in a specific barangay. It's a general-purpose document certifying your good standing in the community.

name: Barangay Clearance
shortDescription: Certifies that you have no derogatory records or cases in your barangay. It is required for jobs or permits.

details.longDescription: This document certifies that the applicant is a resident of the barangay and has no pending cases or negative records within the community. It is often a prerequisite for obtaining other permits, like a Mayor's Permit or a business permit.

name: First-Time Job Seeker
shortDescription: First-time job seekers get government papers without paying fees.

details.longDescription: Under Republic Act 11261, first-time job seekers are exempt from paying fees for certain government-issued documents. This certificate from the barangay is the first step to availing this benefit.

name: Cedula
shortDescription: Community Tax Certificate. An official document required for various transactions.

details.longDescription: The Community Tax Certificate (CTC), or Cedula, is a proof of identity and residence required for many government transactions.
